purpose: The provenance ledger. For every class of architectural claim in this KB: how it was derived, what would falsify it, and how far it can be trusted.

evidence_classes.AST: produced by a whole-tree AST pass over all 127 modules of src/fanops. The STRONGEST class for any 'nothing does X' / 'exactly N do X' claim.
evidence_classes.EXECUTED: a controlled experiment was RUN against a temp root. The strongest class for any claim about LIBRARY BEHAVIOUR (pydantic, SQLite) or RACE OUTCOME.
evidence_classes.SOURCE: read directly from source at fcffa73, cited file:line.
evidence_classes.LIVE_LEDGER: read READ-ONLY (sqlite mode=ro) from the operator's real ledger.
evidence_classes.INFERRED: derived from other evidence; no direct observation.
evidence_classes.UNKNOWN: recorded in unknowns.json. Never presented as fact.

the_authority_order_applied: executable code > executed experiment > AST census > prior-cycle JSON > prior-cycle prose > code comments > documentation. *** Documentation was used as a LEAD, NEVER as proof. Where docs and code disagreed, code won and the disagreement is recorded as a finding. ***

cycle_5_derivations.claim: 127 modules; 528 compile-time import edges; 323 in-function (lazy) edges; 6 typing-only edges.
cycle_5_derivations.method: scratchpad/c5_deps.py — imports classified by SYNTACTIC POSITION (module level / inside a FunctionDef / under `if TYPE_CHECKING:` / inside try-except ImportError).
cycle_5_derivations.falsifiable_by: any module-level fanops import the parser misclassified. Re-run the script.

cycle_5_derivations.claim: EXACTLY ONE compile-time module cycle: {personas, persona_store, persona_research}.
cycle_5_derivations.evidence_class: AST (Tarjan SCC over the module-level edge set)
cycle_5_derivations.falsifiable_by: a second SCC of size > 1 in the compile-time graph.

cycle_5_derivations.claim: The full graph (compile + lazy) contains a 45-module SCC; all 19 subsystems collapse into ONE SCC.

*** 107 of 323 lazy edges point to an EQUAL-OR-HIGHER LEVEL — 56 STRICTLY UPWARD (true layering inversions) + 51 LATERAL (same-level deferred imports). *** Legal only because the import is deferred to call time.
cycle_5_derivations.method: level(SCC) = longest-path depth over the SCC-CONDENSATION of the compile-time graph (a DAG by construction); every module inherits its SCC's level. An edge a->b is 'upward' iff level(b) > level(a), 'lateral' iff equal.
cycle_5_derivations.known_limitation: *** The condensed graph CANNOT contain a violation by construction — level is DEFINED by it. This metric is therefore only meaningful for the LAZY edges, which is exactly what it is used for. Stated so no reader over-reads it. ***
cycle_5_derivations.supersedes: COUP-09 (Cycle 2), which recorded NINE such imports.
cycle_5_derivations.confidence: CERTAIN for the COUNT; the INTENT distribution is UNKNOWN (UNK-C5-2)
cycle_5_derivations.superseded_v1_value: 106 (produced by an entry-order-dependent cycle-cutting DFS — see C5-SC-3)

cycle_5_derivations.claim: The 7-subsystem compile-time cycle (registry/agent-gate/selection/render/caption/metrics/learning) is REAL, not a boundary artifact.
cycle_5_derivations.method: *** An initial partition produced a 13-subsystem cycle. Each closing edge was traced to its MODULE-level source. THREE proved to be MIS-DRAWN BOUNDARIES (paths_rebase->post.media, transcribe->stage_lock, moment_hook_learning->variant_learning) and the partition was CORRECTED. The 7-subsystem cycle is what SURVIVES that correction. ***
cycle_5_derivations.why_this_matters: The brief forbids inventing architecture. A subsystem cycle produced by careless clustering is a FABRICATED violation. This one was checked at module level before being asserted.

cycle_5_derivations.claim: The subsystem partition is TOTAL: 127/127 modules, 0 unassigned, 0 double-assigned, 0 ghosts, *** 19 subsystems (S01-S19; S16 = Studio). ***
cycle_5_derivations.scope_caveat: *** SUBSYSTEMS ARE AN ANALYTIC OVERLAY imposed by Cycle 5. They are NOT declared anywhere in the repository and NOTHING enforces them. The MODULES and the IMPORT EDGES are FACTS; the GROUPING is a MODEL. Any claim made at subsystem level must be traced to module-level edges before it is trusted (C5-SC-2). ***
cycle_5_derivations.v1_errata: The v1 KB variously said '18 subsystems', '18 + Studio' and '19'. The correct and only number is 19.

cross_validation_that_the_analyzer_is_co.note: *** The single strongest reason to trust Cycle 5's dependency numbers: the SAME AST pass independently reproduced Cycle 3's censuses EXACTLY, without being told what they were. ***

matches.cycle_3: 15 (list included meta_graph's INJECTABLE get; OMITTED cli.py:183)

cycle_5_self_corrections.what: An earlier Cycle-5 pass reported 39 network call sites.
cycle_5_self_corrections.why_it_was_wrong: The query matched `s.get(...)` and `session.get(...)` on PLAIN DICTS, not just requests.Session. It counted `led.get()`, `info.get()` etc. as network calls.
cycle_5_self_corrections.the_lesson: *** This is Cycle-2's method note biting the cycle that wrote it down: A CENSUS IS ONLY AS GOOD AS ITS QUERY. It is recorded here rather than quietly fixed, because a silently-corrected census is indistinguishable from one that was never wrong — and the next reader deserves to know the failure mode is live. ***

cycle_5_self_corrections.what: An earlier Cycle-5 subsystem partition produced a 13-subsystem compile-time cycle.
cycle_5_self_corrections.why_it_was_wrong: THREE of the closing edges were artifacts of MIS-DRAWN BOUNDARIES, not real inversions: stage_lock (a lock PRIMITIVE) was filed under orchestration; paths_rebase (a RECOVERY utility) under persistence; moment_hook_learning (a LEARNING module) under selection.
cycle_5_self_corrections.corrected_to: a 7-subsystem cycle, with every closing edge traced to its module-level source before being asserted.
cycle_5_self_corrections.the_lesson: *** Aggregating modules into subsystems MANUFACTURES cycles that do not exist at module level. A subsystem-level violation must be traced to its module-level edges BEFORE it is reported, or the audit fabricates the very architecture it was asked not to invent. ***

cycle_5_self_corrections.what: The v1 KB called the compile-time import graph 'an 11-level DAG (modulo the one persona cycle)' and reported 106 upward lazy edges.
cycle_5_self_corrections.why_it_was_wrong: *** A graph containing a cycle is NOT a DAG. *** What was actually computed was a CYCLE-CUTTING DFS depth (`if m in seen: return 0`), which is ENTRY-ORDER-DEPENDENT for nodes inside an SCC. The correct construct is the SCC CONDENSATION (a DAG by construction), on which level is well-defined for every module.
cycle_5_self_corrections.corrected_to: The SCC-CONDENSED compile graph is an 11-level DAG. Recomputing against true condensed levels changed SIX module levels (personas 4->3, caption 5->4, studio.views_review 5->4, studio.views 6->5, studio.actions_approve 7->6, studio.app_routes_live 7->6) and moved the count from 106 to *** 107 ***.
cycle_5_self_corrections.and_it_exposed_a_second_error: *** The single number 106 CONFLATED two different things. Decomposed: 56 STRICTLY UPWARD (true layering inversions) + 51 LATERAL (same-level deferred imports, which are NOT inversions). ***
cycle_5_self_corrections.the_lesson: A derived number inherits the soundness of its DEFINITION. 'Modulo the one cycle' was hand-waving that hid an entry-order-dependent metric.

cycle_5_self_corrections.what: The v1 KB asserted 'the domain core is a compile-time cycle' and 'this is not a defect ... the import graph is telling the truth about the product.'
cycle_5_self_corrections.why_it_was_wrong: *** TWO errors. (1) At MODULE level the domain core is ACYCLIC — 124 of 127 modules are singleton SCCs, and the only compile-time import cycle (personas x3) is UNRELATED to the learning loop. The 7-subsystem cycle exists ONLY in the AGGREGATED view. (2) 'The loop is the product, therefore the wiring is correct' IS A NON SEQUITUR: a feedback loop in data/control flow does NOT require a cycle in the static import graph — it could be closed by the orchestrator or via a shared abstraction. ***
cycle_5_self_corrections.corrected_to: SPLIT into two claims with different confidences. (A) The domain feedback loop is intentional and IS the product — CERTAIN. (B) Whether the mutual-import WIRING that realizes it is intentional — UNKNOWN. The 'not a defect' verdict is RETRACTED.
cycle_5_self_corrections.the_lesson: *** The comfortable inference is the dangerous one. 'It's the product, leave it alone' would have told a future engineer that a design choice was a given. ***

inherited_evidence_reverified_by_cycle_5.note: Cycle 5 did not re-execute the destructive experiments (the restore race, the strand). It RE-DERIVED the structural facts those experiments depend on, by AST.
reverified: restore_snapshot is the SOLE consumer of ledger.lock (AST lock census: ledger.py:551 _file_lock; ledger.py:484/540 store.lock) -> RC-4's premise HOLDS
compress.py:21 is the ONLY mkdtemp and there is NO rmtree touching it (AST) -> RC-10 HOLDS
exactly 2 os.environ write sites -> INV-18 HOLDS
the [framing]/cv2 try-except is the only fail-CLOSED optional import; whisper/flask/certifi/keyring all fail open -> INV-17 HOLDS

coverage_metrics.coverage_of_structural_primitives: *** 100%. Every ledger transaction, lock, network call, subprocess, mkdtemp, rmtree, env read and env write in src/fanops is enumerated. ***
coverage_metrics.modules_never_read: ~27 (variant_*, digest, hookscore/hookcheck, persona_directives/levers/research, text, bands, frames, audio_energy, most studio/views_*)
coverage_metrics.what_the_census_PROVES_about_the_27: *** They contain NO ledger writer, NO lock, NO network call, NO status client and NO subprocess. That is a structural guarantee, not an assumption. *** Their business logic remains unread (UNK-C5-3).
coverage_metrics.the_honest_statement: Cycle 5 achieves 100% STRUCTURAL coverage and ~79% BEHAVIOURAL coverage. Prior cycles could only claim the latter.
coverage_metrics.note_on_G3: *** The runtime CALL graph was NOT derived. Every SCC claim in this KB is about the STATIC POTENTIAL-DEPENDENCY graph (a superset). They BOUND blast radius; they do not ESTABLISH it. ***

what_would_falsify_this_KB: Any module-level fanops import the AST parser misclassified -> re-run scratchpad/c5_deps.py.
A ledger writer, lock, or network call inside one of the 27 unread modules -> would falsify the census, which enumerated all 127.
A second compile-time module cycle -> would falsify the Tarjan SCC.
A production caller of Ledger.add_render -> would REACTIVATE the dormant Render.path writes (IF-1) and falsify the 'Render is never minted' claim.
A path that produces Post(state=published, public_url='') -> would move AR-08 from LATENT to CRITICAL.

operational_constraint_on_this_cycle.observed_this_cycle: The gate REFUSED a compound shell command merely because it NAMED .orchestration/state/ (a read-only `ls`), and REFUSED a `general-purpose` subagent spawn ('spawn type general-purpose is not allowed during a wave').
operational_constraint_on_this_cycle.consequence: Cycles 1, 2, 3, 4 AND 5 were all executed SINGLE-THREADED. No parallel verification of Cycle 5's dependency claims was possible — the independent verifier this cycle attempted to spawn was refused.
operational_constraint_on_this_cycle.mitigation_applied: Cycle 5 self-verified by CROSS-VALIDATION instead: the same AST pass independently reproduced four of Cycle 3's censuses exactly. That is weaker than an independent agent, and it is recorded as such.
operational_constraint_on_this_cycle.disengage: `orchestrate.py stop` — AN OPERATOR ACTION, not a code change. It remains the single largest constraint on this audit's throughput.
